title: 12L Full-INT4 (MLP + Attn) + BigramHash(4096) — val_bpb 1.1672
body:
Summary

- Novel group INT4 nibble-packing applied to both MLP and attention weights: 2 values per byte, per-group (gs=64) fp16 scales — 0.5 bytes/param vs 0.75 for INT6
- Freed space enables 12 transformer layers (vs 10 in prior art) with U-Net skip connections
- 10% magnitude pruning before quantisation creates zero runs that zstd-22 compresses efficiently
- Final size: 14.4 MB (1.6 MB under budget)
- val_bpb: 1.1672 (post-quant roundtrip, sliding window stride=64, 8×H100 10 min)

Status: Valid
### Results

| Run                                              | val_bpb | Size   | Valid              |
|--------------------------------------------------|--------:|--------|--------------------|
| Intermediate (INT4 MLP + INT6 Attn + bigram 10240) | 1.1462  | 17.5 MB | No — over budget   |
| This submission (full INT4 + bigram 4096)         | 1.1672  | 14.4 MB | Yes                |


Novel Contribution

Standard INT5-in-int8 stores one value per byte, wasting 3 bits. Our group INT4 scheme packs two values per byte with localised per-group scaling:

1. Divide weight matrix rows into groups of 64 columns
2. Per-group scale: max(|group|) / 7.0 stored as fp16
3. Quantise to symmetric [−8, 7], pack 2 values per byte (low nibble = even index, high nibble = odd index)

~417 KB per MLP weight vs 789 KB (INT5) — 47% smaller per matrix

Extending the same scheme to attention projections (Q/K/V/O, all shapes divisible by gs=64) costs ~0.021 bpb relative to INT6 but frees enough budget to run 12 layers instead of 10. Roundtrip MSE on attention weights is ~4–6 × 10⁻⁶, confirming the precision loss is negligible.

Test Plan

- INT4 pack/unpack roundtrip verified exact
- Full quantise→dequantise MSE: ~4.6 × 10⁻⁶
- Training completes on 8×H100 in 10 min (5,476 steps)
- Fits under 16 MB budget (14.4 MB)
